I can’t say if the main sourcing is pharma or compounded pharmacies, but I ask. Typically the response is “compounded”.
(Not gray or black market).

A less discussed fact (that I’ll cover on the HLP soon): a huge component of the peptide market is not gym going guys but women. Especially sharing of GLPs & Reta. I hear from female audience (about 45% of HLP listenership is female) all the time w/health/beauty peptide Qs.
I say this knowing many people will jump in, both feet, regardless. “What should I take?” is America’s anthem.

Rule 1 regarding Rx stimulants, HRT, peptides, supplements, psychedelics, whatever:

“Better living through chemistry still requires better living”

= You still have to do the right things. 

In fact, that’s the place to start. 

And in many cases, stay.
Oh and NIH unlikely to fund this, they are stretched a little ahem, thin, right now.

And once again, I don’t have any stake in any company that sells BPC. It would just be nice to get some actual human data. Yes I know there are a few published human studies, but let’s be honest, those are weak sauce.

I can’t see any reason why a proper multi site RCT of BPC157 done by a few independent groups wouldn’t help clarify any real vs placebo effects. The challenge: no one wants to pony up the $ (more like $$$$ to do it right) but if it were done, what endpoints would you want to see?

For every older brother a male has from the same mother his probability of being gay increases. This is thought to be due to a trace in their moms immune system that governs hormone effects on the brain. But note: it’s a reflection of more not less testosterone exposure in utero. https://t.co/itWCnWq66M

At a biological level sexual preference appears to have a pro-male or pro-female preference circuit and an anti-male (aversion to) or anti-female avoidance circuit. Explains many human observations. Early hormone exposure establishes these. https://t.co/UJExPGJe0a

Exposure to testosterone in utero biases later sexual orientation. So does the number of older brothers a male has. These are robust and replicated effects. They also bias certain aspects of bodily development. Covered in this episode. Also how hormones shape sexual preference. https://t.co/UJExPGJe0a

Public health discourse needs a nomenclature committee before this “peptides” thing gets out of hand. So many types, so much variation in evidence, so much at stake. Common language would really help everyone: pro and against.

Prioritize training over protein count obsession (yes nutrition still matters but putting training first anchors the nutrition aspect). @foundmyfitness on the Huberman Lab podcast out now https://t.co/vQ9eIWHyF8

My reasons for posting “predictions” = what once was a niche space is changing. Peptide Sciences (gray market giant) gone, Nova & HIMS partnering (I find that interesting & important re how it will impact the broader market), general interest in (non)GLP peptides skyrocketing.

I’ve been posting a lot about peptides and some of the health sociology and rules around them. A few people understandably assumed I am being paid for it. I’m not paid by any pharmaceutical company or peptide company. If that were to change, I of course, would make it known.
Also, the market with women is at least as big as the market with men, perhaps even greater.

HRT went from niche and cloaked (steroids in Hollywood & sports ) to HRT (mainstream in parallel wRCTs). Peptides were ushered in from both sides: GLPs w/RCTs (yes I know it was niche long before) and BPC from TikTok. Now Pharma and compounders are grabbing the market.

I predict peptides will change everything re  public health discourse for health & disease. B/c they’re closer to medications (in many cases actual medications!) than supplements but they entered the picture in supplement-like fashion. HRT went the other direction. Get educated.

I covered peptides a while back and I’ll do it again on the HLP because there’s a lot of updates but there’s also this guest episode with a MD expert in them for men and women: if you’re trying to get educated on peptides, may want to give it a listen https://t.co/QdW0PAZyWV

Prediction: the peptides that will hit the broader male and female HRT space next are kisspeptin and gonadorelin (and not in place of HCG!). Will be interesting to see which companies compound for wide distribution first. These are potent and MD monitoring will be key.

In all seriousness, the sunlight piece is probably the most underestimated in terms of its impact on health. It’s the easiest no matter where you live. It costs nothing. But people tend to resist it because it requires you meet the suns schedule. But oh so worth it.

And yes sunlight and sleep and nutrition and quality social connection (per your needs and wants) all vital too. First to say “what about sunlight and sleep etc” wins the “you have too much time on your hands” award and a MRI referral
https://t.co/D3H1ogbaYV

“Repeated exercise produces robust physiological benefits & is the leading lifestyle intervention for human health.”

-Kindel et al, Neuron 2026 

= obvious to many now but significant b/c it’s the 1st line of a traditional scientific study. 5 years ago no one would say it.

And if you wake up before the sun comes out, you’ll have to wait like everyone else… 
And I won’t give specific minute recommendations because it varies by place and time of year. And people have different schedules. It’s cumulative so get what you can!

It’s obvious once you hear it, but the reason to get outside and view sunlight for *longer* on overcast days = less long wavelength light & those days people tend to be indoors more. Get the most you can in the first hour after waking.

To be clear, no one actually believes that people buying from gray market are doing research at home. That was a loophole. That loophole is closed or is closing fast. Compounding pharmacies are going to be proliferating. Provided safe, these are going to keep costs down & black

“Not for human use for research purposes only”= GRAY market. People doing non human research at home? In vitro? Nah.
0n animals? = illegal (animal welfare act). Crackdown on gray & black markets is starting now. Compounding pharmacies are here to stay. Expect more by the week.

A common quote misattribution that needs to be corrected.

“Fire together, wire together.” 
Are NOT the words of Donald Hebb
 (as people mistakenly claim). 

Dr. Carla Shatz is the one who said that first. (in reference to activity dependent wiring of the developing brain.)
They may disagree on specific points but the notion that any one of these silos are both necessary and sufficient for optimal health is frankly, silly.

A very large number of people trust credentialed MDs but *also* self elected blood work & exercise & nutrition adjusted to one’s needs & supplements & many are peptide curious too (not just GLPs). Any post or article about these camps being at odds = out of touch w/the majority.
